pattern: Sorting once exposes both the k largest and k smallest values as the two ends of the same array, turning the problem into a pair of slice sums and a subtraction.
hints: Sort the array once; the k largest values and the k smallest values are then just the last k and first k entries.
Sum each of those two slices separately rather than trying to track running extremes while scanning.
Watch what happens when k equals the whole array or when the array has only one distinct value -- the two sums, and therefore the difference, can legitimately be equal.

edge_inputs.description: all elements identical, difference must be zero regardless of k
k equal to the full array length, so both sums use every element and must be equal
duplicate values straddling the k-way split
smallest array, k equal to its only element
values at the maximum allowed magnitude

stress.description: random arrays and k across the full constraint range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 100)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [nums, k]
